seoTitle: How to Add a WhatsApp Button to Your Wix Site
metaDescription: Learn how to add a floating click-to-chat WhatsApp button to your Wix website so visitors can message you instantly.
intro: A WhatsApp click-to-chat button gives visitors an instant way to reach out without filling out a form. This guide covers building the WhatsApp link with your country code, binding it to a button or icon, and pinning it to a fixed corner of the screen so it stays visible as visitors browse.

steps.heading: Choose a placement zone
steps.body: Decide where on your page or header the WhatsApp button will sit for maximum visibility.

steps.heading: Add a button or icon
steps.body: From the Add Elements sidebar, select a vector icon, image badge, or text button for the chat action.

steps.heading: Build your WhatsApp link
steps.body: Structure a click-to-chat URL including your country dial code and an optional pre-filled welcome message.

steps.heading: Link the element
steps.body: Use the element's link settings to bind your WhatsApp URL directly to the graphic or button.

steps.heading: Pin it to the screen
steps.body: Enable the Pin to Screen option so the button stays fixed in place as visitors scroll.

steps.heading: Style it and test on mobile
steps.body: Match the button's colors to your brand, check mobile placement, and test the click-to-chat flow before publishing.

faq.question: Do I need the WhatsApp Business app for this to work?
faq.answer: No, a basic click-to-chat link works with any WhatsApp account, though a Business account can offer added features separately.

faq.question: Can I pre-fill a welcome message in the chat?
faq.answer: Yes, the click-to-chat link format supports a custom pre-filled text message that appears when the visitor opens the chat.

faq.question: Will the button stay visible while scrolling?
faq.answer: Yes, if you enable the Pin to Screen setting, the button stays fixed in a corner as visitors scroll the page.

faq.question: Do I need to include my country code in the link?
faq.answer: Yes, the WhatsApp API link format requires the full international country code for the number to route correctly.
